content.title: Daybreak: Tools for securing every organization in the world
content.summary: OpenAI expands its Daybreak cybersecurity platform with Codex Security updates, the full GPT-5.5-Cyber model release, a new Cyber Partner Program with 28+ security vendors, and the Patch the Planet open-source initiative.
content.summary_1: OpenAI is dramatically expanding Daybreak, its AI-powered cybersecurity platform, with several major announcements on June 22, 2026. The updated Codex Security plugin has already scanned 30M+ commits across 30,000+ codebases, with 500,000+ findings auto-resolved. GPT-5.5-Cyber — now released in full after limited preview — sets new state-of-the-art at 85.6% on CyberGym. A new Daybreak Cyber Partner Program brings in 28 major security vendors including CrowdStrike, Palo Alto Networks, Cisco, Cloudflare, SentinelOne, and Wiz, enabling them to offer GPT-5.5 with Trusted Access for Cyber in their products. OpenAI also establishes government Trusted Access partnerships with Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Japan, South Korea, and EU institutions (ENISA).
content.body_text:
OpenAI is expanding Daybreak to democratize patching at machine speed. The announcement covers multiple pillars:

**Codex Security (updated):** The plugin now supports full defensive workflows — deep scans, attack path tracing, threat model generation, patch creation, and integration with existing vulnerability management systems via SARIF/CodeQL. Stats: 30,000+ repos scanned, 30M+ commits, 500,000+ findings auto-fixed, 70,000+ manually marked resolved.

**GPT-5.5-Cyber (full release):** Previously in limited permissive-only preview, now fully available to verified defenders. Benchmarks: 85.6% CyberGym (vs 81.8% GPT-5.5), 39.5% ExploitGym (vs 25.95%), 69.8% SEC-bench Pro (vs 63.1%). Designed for sustained analysis across large codebases, from vulnerability identification through patch delivery.

**Daybreak Cyber Partner Program:** 28 security companies can now integrate GPT-5.5 with Trusted Access for Cyber into their products. Partners include Accenture, Akamai, CrowdStrike, Cisco, Cloudflare, Darktrace, Elastic, Fortinet, IBM, Okta, Palo Alto Networks, Proofpoint, SentinelOne, Sophos, Tenable, Wiz, Zscaler, and more.

**Government partnerships:** Trusted Access for Cyber established with Australia, Canada, France, Germany, Japan, South Korea, and EU's ENISA.

**Patch the Planet:** Initiative with Trail of Bits, HackerOne, and Calif to patch widely used open-source software.

tech_insights.impact_summary: OpenAI launches a comprehensive AI cybersecurity ecosystem spanning enterprise tools, partner integrations, government alliances, and open-source protection — fundamentally shifting AI's role in cyber defense.
actions_developer: Integrate Codex Security plugin into your CI/CD pipeline for continuous vulnerability scanning
Apply for Trusted Access for Cyber if doing authorized security research requiring GPT-5.5-Cyber
Use Codex Security's SARIF export to integrate AI findings into existing security workflows
actions_business: Security product companies: apply to Daybreak Cyber Partner Program to offer GPT-5.5 to customers
Government/critical infrastructure operators: explore Trusted Access for Cyber national partnerships
Enterprise security teams: evaluate Codex Security for automated vulnerability backlog reduction
